in the admin interface, the delete confirmation page is typically implemented by creating a custom form or view that handles the deletion logic. this involves detecting when an object is updated and then triggering a deletion process if the object is no longer in use. the confirmation page is usually rendered when the user attempts to save an object that has been modified, and it prompts them to confirm the deletion of dependent objects. this is done by checking if the object is out of date and if any related objects are affected. the confirmation page is a crucial part of the admin interface as it ensures data integrity and prevents accidental deletions.
